key_points: Bay State Road dorms generate constant noise complaints from residents
Freshman housing is random; many end up far from main campus
Limited info on dorm quality in public student discourse
key_quotes: Every fucking night when I'm doing my work some doofus is being loud and obnoxious
MOBS of you idiots screaming up and down Bay State and Comm Ave
People LIVE HERE AND LIKE TO SLEEP

key_points: Campus is open and urban—city IS the social scene, not just campus
Greek life exists but is not dominant; social scene is fragmented
Freshmen struggle to find their footing; friend groups shift dramatically after year one
key_quotes: my friend group completely changed after year 1, met a lot of great people
Not traditional sport bond spirit campus—more of small knit communities where everyone do their own thing
walk with purpose on comm ave... switch up the going out outfits, the jeans and tube tops are uncanny

key_points: Bay State Underground, Late Night @ West, and Law School Cafe were closed with no warning
Target near campus stocks expired food—students explicitly warned to check dates
Dining options have been quietly reduced even as tuition rises
key_quotes: Bay State Underground, Late Night @ West, and Law School Cafe have been closed... this sucks
They stock expired food products, drugs, and personal health products, so be careful
this is what i'm paying for meanwhile BU is having a hiring freeze and still providing unlivable conditions

key_points: Multiple suicide incidents at Warren Towers reported; mental health resources exist but awareness is patchy
Sexual assault survivors report BU's Title IX process as retraumatizing and ineffective
One student describes BU years as 'the worst and most traumatic of my life'
key_quotes: Title IX cases are supposed to last 3 months, mine went for over half a year, only for them to say there wasn't enough evidence
0 cases have actually gone in favor of the victim
BU has some exceptional mental health services—they really invest in your wellbeing

key_points: Sticker price now approaching $94k/year—students openly calling it unjustifiable for a top-40 school
Financial aid is strong for low-income students but brutal for middle class
BU WILL negotiate aid packages upward if you ask—one student got $15k added after requesting more
key_quotes: No scholarship for me so 77k per year ain't feasible
BU is charging 94 RACKS for let's face it a decent (not even that good) of a private school that's top 40
Boston University added $15,000 EXTRA TO MY FINANCIAL AID OFFER AFTER I REQUESTED MORE AID

key_points: Grade deflation reputation is real but debated—current students say it's overblown by outsiders
Professors are described as excellent by many, especially in STEM and neuroscience
Hub general education requirements are confusing enough that a student built a tracking tool to navigate them
key_quotes: BU is a rigorous school. Professors are fair. You get the grade you deserve. There is less inflation at BU
I got tired of tracking Hub requirements in a spreadsheet, so I built Terrier Tracker
tons of amazing and successful professors. i really enjoy walking around Boston as well

key_points: Admin repeatedly criticized for spineless non-responses to campus controversies
Staff layoffs of ~120 positions announced while faculty got promotions the same week
TerrierGPT AI tool launched before fixing the notoriously inaccurate bus schedule—widely mocked
key_quotes: FYI they've had over 40 meetings to discuss these new values and not a thing has actually happened
Can't believe we got TerrierGPT before accurate BU Bus schedule
BU admin doesn't do anything. Last year a majority voted for BU to disclose investments and divest—President ignored it

key_points: Campus runs along Commonwealth Ave—open, urban, and beautiful but with no traditional quad feel
Boston location is a massive asset: internships, culture, other schools nearby
Construction noise is a persistent, years-long complaint
key_quotes: Construction—very noisy
Campus is cohesive. A lot of things to do. Harvard and MIT very close by. Many opportunities for internship and jobs
The scenic view of MIT's back... Boston University: The scenic view of MIT's back

key_points: Boston location drives strong internship access across virtually every industry
Alumni network is described as helpful IF you stay in Boston metro area
Business and COM schools have strong industry connections; outcomes vary sharply by major
key_quotes: After graduation, your fellow alumni will drop things into your lap as long as you are willing to work hard
If you choose to stay in the Boston area, you can make a lot of money, but be aware there is a rivalry with the other schools and Big H usually wins a lot of the time
Art got a job down the street from BU at the Museum of Fine Arts as a museum curator

key_points: Cost has jumped from ~$75k to ~$94k in just a few years with no clear quality improvement
Students explicitly questioning whether BU is worth it at current prices
Scholarships exist and are negotiable—but middle-class families get squeezed hardest
key_quotes: When I first started it was $75k a year and you're telling me it's 20 more thousand dollars? Just keeping it real if you're thinking about transferring, do it
BU would cost me $90k a year... unfortunately I will not be attending BU which was my dream school
Unfortunately, BU would cost me $90k a year and Villanova would cost $75k a year. This is way out of my price range

key_points: Students who love Boston love BU; those who wanted a 'college campus' feel often regret it
Strong sense of community within specific schools/programs, weaker university-wide identity
Political tensions are high and recent—ICE controversy consumed campus in 2025
key_quotes: ED Not Going Your Way—Got into BU, and now I go here and I couldn't be happier and see myself anywhere else
It has a 90%+ female fanbase
I know you had a lot of ups and downs during your time at BU, but you are through it and ready to move on

key_points: Title IX: survivors report zero successful outcomes, process described as protecting BU not victims
Financial: cost rose $20k in a few years while dining venues close and staff get laid off
Administration: 40+ meetings on stated values with zero action; admin silence on major campus incidents
key_quotes: bu does not care about assault... 0 cases have actually gone in favor of the victim
i'm livid. this is what i'm paying for meanwhile BU is having a hiring freeze and still providing unlivable conditions
I have recently learned that the following would be the best way to share your concerns: pr@bu.edu—[admin response to ICE controversy]

key_points: Low-income students often get exceptional aid packages including full rides
Boston itself is a hidden gem—access to MIT, Harvard, museums, and endless internships
Individual professors frequently cited as exceptional, especially in neuroscience and STEM
key_quotes: If you're low income, BU gives really great need aid. So many of my low income friends got full rides
My FAVORITE neuro professor at BU. Literally hugged him at graduation on stage
Art got a job down the street from BU at the Museum of Fine Arts as a museum curator
